mentions.note: Yielding 3%, a magical level. Fed rate cuts now unlikely, so stock depressed. Long-term holder since $30 should buy more. Stock at 20x spring-selling-season earnings.

Disappointing: 0.6% SSS (below exp.), modest beats, reiterated FY guidance. Needs lower rates to thrive; early May showed strength. Owned in charitable trust as rate-cut hedge; if Fed cuts rates, will regret pre-emptive sale.
Held for charitable trust; interest-rate sensitive housing exposure; huge bust but only bad quarter could crater further; comparable setups improve in late cycle.

Purchased as hedge to expected rate cuts under new Fed chief; backfired as rates rose instead; 30-year Treasury yield now 5%+ kills rate-sensitive housing plays; demonstrates bond market veto power over equity rallies.
Eviscerated because market no longer believes in rate cuts; needs housing turnover which won't happen with war uncertainty; 1999 rate-cut proxy.
Template stock for rate-cut environment; owned since 1987 Elmont opening. Provides portfolio balance during tech selloffs like today.
Hit 2-year low yesterday but surged 5% on ceasefire optimism; pent-up housing demand should drive paint/DIY purchases on rate cuts.
Down 2.4%, hitting new low; Cramer calls it 'awful stock'; home improvement weakness is major canary for consumer health.
Up; one of few retail winners but sector showing weakness overall.
Stuck due to frozen housing market tied to high rates; Cramer holds for charitable trust awaiting rate-cut catalyst; could be big winner if Fed cuts.
Greenland peace dividend benefits home improvement retailer; up $953 or 2.5% on tariff relief.
Rallied again but outdone by Lowe's; housing recovery benefiting category; holding strength.
Home improvement benefiting from lower rates; deskbot has actual buyers and lows flirt with new highs.
Preferred housing exposure over Fortune Brands; historically rallies ahead of Fed rate cuts; Cramer's recommended housing lever.
